"John Bishop, I think we are not recording to the X-T cards" — videographer reference to the storage media (likely SD cards or P2 cards); "X-T" is Whisper's rendering of acronym/letters; common-noun

"the marches by the Ku Klux Klan. From a child's point of view, every dress through Lincolnville" — Whisper-garble; speaker may be saying "every [day they] dressed through Lincolnville" (referring to KKK robe-marches) or "every-week" or similar; preserved as common-noun ambiguity

pending_context.whisper: the Haven and Central Avenue, which is now Martin Luther King Avenue
pending_context.notes: "we went down on the corner of the Haven in Central Avenue, which is now Martin Luther King Avenue" — canonical Lincolnville historic St. Augustine streets; Central Avenue was renamed Martin Luther King Jr. Avenue; "Haven" is Whisper for an unverified cross-street (possibly "Bravo" or "Bridge" or "Eastern Avenue")

"the doctor's building on trimmer's in street" — canonical St. Augustine downtown street; "trimmer's in" is Whisper-garble for an unverified street name (possibly Cordova, Carver, Treasury, or Granada); same as Pass 1 #51.8

"our school closed down and we had to go to our street school" — Duncan may be saying "the white school" or naming a specific St. Augustine school; "our street school" likely a Whisper rendering of "Orange Street school" (Orange Street School is a canonical St. Augustine school where forced integration happened); spelling unverified

Same as Pass 1 #51.10 with additional context: "Mrs. Cara Johnson refers to it as like a hidden vapor, you know, over this town"; canonical St. Augustine 40th ACCORD co-founder; spelling confirmed as Cora (not Cara) per the St. Augustine local-historian record

"guess speaker was Sonja Plummer. She was a granddaughter of one of the Civil Rights activists here, Reverend Goldie U-Banks" — canonical Sonja Plummer-Morgan, granddaughter of Rev. Goldie Eubanks Sr. (St. Augustine NAACP youth-council founder); spelling not yet fully verified
Same as Pass 1 #51.28; appears in "It was on the Soto place" — uncertain Anastasia Island restaurant

"Miss Cara Johnson and I set up a meeting at the Gallimor Center" — canonical St. Augustine community center; spelling not yet verified — possibly Galimore (a local family name) or a misheard "Solomon Calhoun Community Center" (the canonical Lincolnville center); preserved as uncertain canonical

"everybody looked at you as though you had a communicable disease" — Duncan's child-eye account of the white-medical-office reception of Black patients after the desegregation sign came down; preserved as speaker-originating affective description, not a Whisper error

"Mrs. Cara Johnson refers to it as like a hidden vapor, you know, over this town" — Cora Johnson's canonical Lincolnville-era metaphor for the post-Movement St. Augustine racial atmosphere; speaker-originating cultural reference
Same context as P2.32; canonical co-honoree on the Foot Soldiers Monument; spelling/identification not yet verified

Same as Pass 1 #51.17 — canonical defense contractor that preceded Northrop Grumman at the St. Augustine site; "Fertile Hill" is Whisper for "Fairchild" (consonant approximation) // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]

"his wife is a professional, an internal medicine. She has a Indian internal medicine" — Whisper-garbled; the speaker is describing her daughter-in-law as an Indian-American internal-medicine physician; preserved as ambiguous common-noun
Same as Pass 1 #51.15; Brown University academic; flag as speaker-originating until spelling verified

"We found her all the way over in Africa, Medagascar to Allen on the side" — canonical US Ambassador to Madagascar in the Clinton era; the speaker may be saying "to Atlanta" or "to Allen [Iolanda]" — Iolanda Allen was a Black US Ambassador to Madagascar; spelling not yet verified

"we have, uh, the chairman, Richard Burton out of Jacksonville, who was a national NAACP board member" — canonical St. Augustine 40th ACCORD chairman Richard Burton (no relation to the actor); speaker-originating accurate identification
